taskDescription: Click on the key anatomical terms related to the lungs and trachea in the text below.
textField:
The *trachea* extends from the *cricoid* cartilage at C6 to the *carina* at T4/T5, where it divides into the right and left main *bronchi*. It has 16-20 *C-shaped* cartilage rings open posteriorly, bridged by the *trachealis* muscle. The right main *bronchus* is wider, shorter, and more *vertical* than the left, making it the common site for *aspiration* of foreign bodies.

The right lung has three *lobes* (upper, middle, lower) separated by the *horizontal* and *oblique* fissures, with 10 *bronchopulmonary* segments. The left lung has two lobes separated by only the oblique fissure. The *lingula* of the left lung corresponds to the middle lobe of the right. Each bronchopulmonary segment is a functionally independent unit with its own *segmental* bronchus and artery.

The *pleura* consists of *visceral* and *parietal* layers with the *pleural* cavity between them. The *hilum* is the area where the bronchus, pulmonary artery, and pulmonary veins enter the lung. The *intercostal* *neurovascular* bundle runs in the *costal* groove along the inferior border of each rib, which is why needle insertion for *thoracocentesis* must be over the upper border of the lower rib.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review lung and tracheal anatomy terminology.
Good effort! Some key terms were missed.
Excellent! You identified the key respiratory anatomy terms.
